findings.text: solution.py docstring independently re-derives all four coefficients (A, B, C, D) — matches log.md; both agree on f(x)=sin(x)+0.1*x^2.
evaluator.py accepts --seed but generate_test_data() ignores it, so all three seeds return identical MSE=0 (evaluator quirk, not a solution defect).
Closed-form deterministic; seed invariance expected. All three eval seeds return identical MSE=0.
Held-out MSE bar invisible on symlog (exact zero).
findings.fix: Annotate exact-zero instead of plotting empty bar.

findings.text: No external SR benchmark paper cited. Advisory only.
suspicious_improvement=true is fully explained: solution IS the exact ground-truth generator function. Not metric gaming.
Evaluator --seed is silently ignored; three-seed eval is three identical runs.

synopsis: Orbit 01-sinusoid-quadratic correctly identifies f(x) = sin(x) + 0.1*x^2 by inspection, achieving exact zero MSE on the held-out test set. Code, derivation, and log prose are mutually consistent with no constraint violations. Only non-blocking figure polish issues remain.
